2604.25562.title: SnapGuard: Lightweight Prompt Injection Detection for Screenshot-Based Web Agents
2604.25562.paper_summary: Web agents have emerged as an effective paradigm for automating interactions with complex web environments, yet remain vulnerable to prompt injection attacks that embed malicious instructions into webpage content to induce unintended actions. This threat is further amplified for screenshot-based web agents, which operate on rendered visual webpages rather than structured textual representations, making predominant text-centric defenses ineffective. Although multimodal detection methods have been explored, they often rely on large vision-language models (VLMs), incurring significant computational overhead. The bottleneck lies in the complexity of modern webpages: VLMs must comprehend the global semantics of an entire page, resulting in substantial inference time and GPU memory usage. This raises a critical question: can we detect prompt injection attacks from screenshots in a lightweight manner? In this paper, we observe that injected webpages exhibit distinct characteristics compared to benign ones from both visual and textual perspectives. Building on this insight, we propose SnapGuard, a lightweight yet accurate method that reformulates prompt injection detection as multimodal representation analysis over webpage screenshots. SnapGuard leverages two complementary signals: a visual stability indicator that identifies abnormally smooth gradient distributions induced by malicious content, and action-oriented textual signals recovered via contrast-polarity reversal. Extensive evaluations across eight attacks and two benign settings demonstrate that SnapGuard achieves an F1 score of 0.75, outperforming GPT-4o-prompt while being 8x faster (1.81s vs. 14.50s) and introducing no additional memory overhead.
2604.25562.paper_summary_zh: 網路代理已成為自動化與複雜網路環境互動的有效範式，但仍然容易受到提示注入攻擊的威脅，這種攻擊會將惡意指令嵌入網頁內容中，以誘導未預期的行為。對於基於截圖的網路代理來說，這一威脅進一步加劇，因為它們在渲染的視覺網頁上運作，而不是在結構化的文本表示上，這使得以文本為中心的防禦措施無效。儘管已經探索了多模態檢測方法，但它們通常依賴於大型視覺-語言模型（VLMs），這會產生顯著的計算開銷。瓶頸在於現代網頁的複雜性：VLMs 必須理解整個頁面的全球語義，這導致了大量的推理時間和 GPU 記憶體使用。這引發了一個關鍵問題：我們能否以輕量級的方式從截圖中檢測提示注入攻擊？在本文中，我們觀察到，注入的網頁在視覺和文本兩個方面與良性網頁顯示出明顯的特徵。基於這一見解，我們提出了 SnapGuard，一種輕量且準確的方法，將提示注入檢測重新定義為對網頁截圖的多模態表示分析。SnapGuard 利用兩個互補信號：一個視覺穩定性指標，用於識別由惡意內容引起的異常平滑梯度分佈，以及通過對比極性反轉恢復的以行動為導向的文本信號。在八種攻擊和兩種良性設置下的廣泛評估表明，SnapGuard 實現了 0.75 的 F1 分數，超越了 GPT-4o-prompt，同時速度快了 8 倍（1.81 秒對比 14.50 秒），且未引入額外的記憶體開銷。
2604.25562.author: Mengyao Du et.al.
2604.25562.authors: Mengyao Du, Han Fang, Haokai Ma, Jiahao Chen, Kai Xu, Quanjun Yin, Ee-Chien Chang
